description: The mother recorded for a birth. It identifies the animal recognized as the dam of the newborn in that birth.

given: a second animal that is not the dam
then: birth_mother of the birth log with other=the dam == true
birth_mother of the birth log with other=the second animal == false
when: record_birth for the newborn animal with mother set to the dam animal

probe_semantics: Deliver whether a given animal is the one recorded as the mother on the birth log, so an assertion can confirm the recorded dam against an expected animal.

punts: kind='assertion' guessed deterministically from gap_kind='bundle_field'
field declared in a PHP attribute plugin's buildFieldDefinitions, not a YAML file

SHAPE DEVIATION from the propose-terms sketch: the proposal read 'deliver the animal recorded as the mother'; implemented instead as the has_parent house form for an entity reference — an assertion over an `other` animal delivering a BOOLEAN (does the birth log's recorded mother == other?). A raw per-run asset UUID cannot reproduce across runs or ports, so it could never score; the boolean-against-other is the only reproducible, scorable form and matches the one existing entity-reference probe (has_parent).
subject is a birth LOG (subject_kind=event); the mother is read off the birth log's own `mother` relationship, not off the child asset's `parent` relationship (that is what has_parent already covers)
proposed by openai:gpt-5.6-terra (propose-terms); PROVISIONAL until first_pack_seal is filled by a real sealed recording

description: The laboratory specimen category recorded on a lab test log, such as soil, tissue, or water.

then: lab_sample_type of the lab test log == soil
lab_sample_type of a second lab test log recorded with no lab_test_type == the empty value
when: record_log of kind lab_test against the land asset with lab_test_type set to soil

probe_semantics: Deliver the sample category recorded on the subject lab-test log (the lab_test_type value, e.g. soil, tissue, or water), or an empty value when none was recorded.

punts: SHAPE DEVIATION from the propose-terms sketch: the proposal guessed kind='entity' (deterministically, from gap_kind='allowed_values') and its discriminating flow read 'lab_sample_type == soil' as if the term named a standalone entity. Implemented instead as a value-delivering ASSERTION over the log's own lab_test_type attribute — the material_type_recorded / material_quantity house form (a value-delivering assertion on a recorded log). An entity term has no recordable observable here: farmOS states the sample category as a list_string ATTRIBUTE on the log, not as a referenced entity, so the reproducible, scorable value is the recorded category string itself.

the list members soil/tissue/water are config-entity-driven allowed values (farm_lab_test.lab_test_type.* config entities via FarmLabTestHelper::labTestTypeAllowedValues); they are NOT gated against a glossary closed set — the value flows in as a free input string and the source's own allowed-values function validates it at the boundary (validated live: 'soil' accepted and delivered verbatim)
subject is a lab_test LOG (subject_kind=event); the value read is the log's lab_test_type attribute, delivered verbatim by the boundary

description: The laboratory that performed a recorded laboratory test.

then: laboratory of the lab test log == the recorded laboratory name
laboratory of a second lab test log recorded with no lab == the empty value
when: record_log of kind lab_test against the land asset with lab set to a laboratory name

probe_semantics: Deliver the name of the laboratory recorded as having performed the subject lab test (the log's lab reference), or an empty value when none was recorded.

punts: kind='assertion' guessed deterministically from gap_kind='bundle_field'; field declared in a PHP attribute plugin's buildFieldDefinitions, not a YAML file

SHAPE DEVIATION from the propose-terms sketch: the proposal read 'deliver the laboratory ... as the laboratory value itself'. Implemented as the recorded lab term's NAME (a reproducible string), read off the log's single-valued `lab` entity_reference (target taxonomy_term--lab, auto_create) — the material_type_recorded name-readback house form. A raw per-run taxonomy term UUID could never reproduce across runs or ports and so could never score; the term's own stated name is the reproducible, scorable value. The reference is single-valued (validated live: lab rel delivered as one object, not a list), so the value is a scalar name, not a list.
subject is a lab_test LOG (subject_kind=event); the value read follows the log's lab reference to the term's stated name
proposed by openai:gpt-5.6-terra (propose-terms); PROVISIONAL until first_pack_seal is filled by a real sealed recording

description: The testing methods recorded on a lab test's measurement quantity, as an ordered list of term names.

then: lab_test_measurement of the lab test log == the recorded method name(s)
lab_test_measurement of a second lab test log recorded with a standard quantity carrying no method == the empty list
when: record_log of kind lab_test against the land asset with a test-classified quantity whose test_method is set to a method name

probe_semantics: Deliver the ordered test_method term names recorded on the first test-classified quantity of the subject lab-test log, or an empty list when the log carries no test measurement or the measurement records no method.

punts: kind='assertion' guessed deterministically from gap_kind='quantity_type'; the quantity bundle field (test_method) lives in the quantity module, outside the log-type file

SHAPE DEVIATION from the propose-terms sketch: the proposal read 'deliver the measured value on the recorded log that is classified as a laboratory test measurement'. Implemented instead as the material_type_recorded house form — the ordered test_method term NAMES recorded on the first test-classified (quantity--test) quantity. The raw measured value is already delivered by quantity_recorded and a bare number cannot distinguish a lab test measurement from any other quantity; the test_method the measurement carries is the reproducible, scorable identity of a lab test measurement (a quantity--test with its method), and names (not per-run term UUIDs) reproduce across runs and ports. This is why the write surface gained QuantitySpec.test_method (MetaCoding-wgy).

SELECTION PUNT (the 'two firsts' caveat, inherited from material_type_recorded review of 74c499f): 'the first test quantity of the log' is OUR unambiguity convention, sound only while a flow carries at most one test-classified quantity per log; a multi-test-quantity flow would need the probe to say WHICH. The bound flows carry exactly one test quantity per log.

subject is a lab_test LOG (subject_kind=event); the value read is the log's own quantity relationship, the first quantity--test's own test_method relationship, and each referenced term's own stated name

punts: kind='assertion' guessed deterministically from gap_kind='bundle_field'; field declared in a PHP attribute plugin's buildFieldDefinitions, not a YAML file

the source's field is 'lab_processed_date'; the term is 'lab_processing_date' (the proposal's name) — the same recorded field, delivered verbatim by the boundary as an ISO-8601 instant (validated live)

TIMESTAMP NOTE: this probe returns an instant, but its value is an absolute ISO-8601 date AUTHORED as a bundle-field input (not the log's effective time and not derived from the recording run's wall clock), so the MetaCoding-bdy relative-offset trap does not apply. add-term does not emit ProbeSpec.returns_timestamp, so it is left False (the codegen default) — accurate for the guard that flag drives, since the flows date this field absolutely and never with a relative offset. Reported as a codegen limitation, not silently hand-edited.
subject is a lab_test LOG (subject_kind=event); the value read is the log's lab_processed_date attribute, delivered verbatim
